output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's Your 60-Second Fix
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and send a legally sound demand letter in 60 seconds for only $19.99.
content_outline: Why landlords withhold security deposits (and when it's outright illegal)
Security deposit deductions: What your landlord can and cannot charge you for
State law deadlines: How long your landlord really has to return your money
The no-lawyer solution: How a demand letter forces your landlord to pay
Next steps if your landlord ignores your demand
key_points: Normal wear and tear is not a valid reason to withhold a security deposit
Most states require landlords to return deposits within 14 to 30 days
A formal letter of demand is often enough to recover your money without court
Deposits-Back.com lets tenants generate a legally sound demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99 with no sign-up or subscription
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds—just $19.99, no sign-up required.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting security deposits

Stop letting your landlord steal your security deposit. Here's the letter that gets it back in 60 seconds.
It's been 45 days. Where's your security deposit?
Illegal. Illegal. Illegal. If your landlord said this, they actually owe you double.
POV: You just moved out and your landlord is ghosting you.

short_form_scripts.hook: Your landlord said they're keeping your deposit for 'cleaning fees'?
short_form_scripts.body: Here's the truth: normal wear and tear is NOT deductible. Scuffed floors? Faded paint? That's on them, not you. But arguing over text won't work.
short_form_scripts.cta: You need a demand letter with teeth. Go to Deposits-Back.com. Generate yours in 60 seconds. Just $19.99. No sign-up. No subscription. Get your money back.

short_form_scripts.hook: It's been 45 days. Where's your security deposit?
short_form_scripts.body: Most states require your deposit back in 14 to 30 days max. After that? They're breaking tenant law—and in many states, you could be owed double damages plus interest.
short_form_scripts.cta: Don't hire a lawyer yet. Hit Deposits-Back.com. $19.99. 60 seconds. Send the legally sound letter that makes landlords pay attention.

short_form_scripts.hook: My landlord won't give me my deposit back. If this is you, stop begging.
short_form_scripts.body: Texting 'please' for the 10th time isn't working. Landlords respond to paper trails, not DMs. A formal demand letter proves you know your rights.
short_form_scripts.cta: Create yours at Deposits-Back.com. No sign-up. No subscription. Just a proven letter that gets results in 60 seconds.
